content: The nix-community hydra is now starting to build the rustdoc make target (currently under the doc jobs, I'm planning to add it to the rust job today). Is there any branch/fork that would need doc builds as well?
Latest rust doc can no be found under: https://hydra.nix-community.org/job/rust-for-linux/rust/doc/latest/download-by-type/doc/manual
Nice! Is that part of a "doc package" for the distro that would go along the kernel packages in Nix?
No

Maybe/hopefully some day. Currently (at least from my point of view) the linux packaging in nixos/nixpkgs is not good. For now it is an external package just for us, nixpkgs does not have documentation for the kernel
BTW, accidentally build the rust kernel with rust 1.53.0, it did not complain at all
I'm trying to add the htmldoc makefile target as a second package, to make development a bit easier. Does it need rustc/rustdoc?
Do you mean the kernel's Sphinx-generated docs? i.e.htmldocs?
yes
I don't think it should need Rust (at least for the moment!).
But you will have to install quite a lot of stuff anyway (Sphinx dependencies etc.).
Though my plan is to wire the Rust docs into the main docs, but that will come later. At that point, it might be the case that the main docs need Rust then (or at least optionally).
I remembered some plans like that, wasn't sure how far that got

Dependencies should by quite trivial. I only have one problem, maybe someone has an idea.
I see the error

linux-doc> ../Documentation/Makefile:39: The '/nix/store/62ssavddxh61k937yskjyj5ms368prxd-python3.9-sphinx-4.0.2/bin/sphinx-build' command was not found. Make sure you have Sphinx installed and in PATH, or set the SPHINXBUILD make variable to point to the full path of the '/nix/store/62ssavddxh61k937yskjyj5ms368prxd-python3.9-sphinx-4.0.2/bin/sphinx-build' executable.

Whereas /nix/store/62ssavddxh61k937yskjyj5ms368prxd-python3.9-sphinx-4.0.2/bin/sphinx-build seems to be a valid executable:

kloenk@durin ~/p/rust-for-nix> /nix/store/62ssavddxh61k937yskjyj5ms368prxd-python3.9-sphinx-4.0.2/bin/sphinx-build                                                                                                                                                                               1
usage: sphinx-build [OPTIONS] SOURCEDIR OUTPUTDIR [FILENAMES...]
sphinx-build: error: the following arguments are required: sourcedir, outputdir, filenames
I already used the SPHINXBUILD make argument to set it to the full path
Interesting, installing which did fix it, Seems like a bug in KBuild to me
@Finn Behrens What alternative to which is there that is always available? whence exists in zsh but not bash. where and whereis aren't always available either I think.
Yeah, in the nix stdenv nothing is available. So as I set the SPHINXBUILD, variable, IHMO it should not need which to check if it is installed
But what should it use to check that it exists then?
The binary itself? There are some bash/makefile checks that work without which.
For now I just added which, works for me :-)
It did build locally (did not look at the html files yet, as I did build it on a headless system). Let's see what hydra thinks of the htmldoc target
Hydra will think it takes a long time to process ;P The Sphinx docs take quite a bit of time to build...
For headless systems, sometimes links/lynx/etc. are enough to see if it worked.
8 minutes, seems okay
I did it: https://hydra.nix-community.org/job/rust-for-linux/doc/htmldoc/latest/download-by-type/doc/manual

Sadly the hydra is not that fast, the kernel itself takes about 37 minutes to build.
So if some company ever want's to help us with servers, hydra would love to take it xD

Finn Behrens said:

I did it: https://hydra.nix-community.org/job/rust-for-linux/doc/htmldoc/latest/download-by-type/doc/manual

(The link will change again this evening, when I merge it into the main rust jobset)
Sadly it does leak /build/source/ on this page, but I guess that is not a big problem
Now the hydra is building emacs, could take some time :-). Hopefully later this day the docs on rust are finished. Should we add both links to the readme of the GitHub project?

Hydra did finish the latest build.
htmldoc is now here: https://hydra.nix-community.org/job/rust-for-linux/rust/htmldoc/latest/download-by-type/doc/manual
and rustdoc is here: https://hydra.nix-community.org/job/rust-for-linux/rust/doc/latest/download-by-type/doc/manual
Those links will update with new pushes to the rust branch of our GitHub repo.
Kernel syncs (updating to the next rc) will most likely break those builds, we will have to see, when it happens
I added to simple nixos vm test as well: https://hydra.nix-community.org/eval/177193#tabs-new
Do we have any other kernel drivers that would be worth testing? Right now I can only think of binder and the samples. I don't know if binder would work on nixos, and how to test it
Already Broke. But not our fault, falsely package in nixpkgs
The nixos/hydra ci vm tests are now succeeding. :tada:
